text: George Washington's Dream (Parker Bros., 1929)
George arrived at New York City on the Fourth of July shortly after he had been elected President of the U.S.A.
He obtained a fine Room at the hotel Admiral and after getting settled, he sees by his Ingersoll that it is nearly Time for Dinner.
He has just got Time to shave and, using his brand new safety Razor, this is soon accomplished.
He looks as fresh as [BLANK] all dressed up like [BLANK].
He takes the Elevator to the Hotel Lobby and sees, to his great Astonishment, his Friend Judge Taft, who is smiling like [BLANK].
They shake hands and George invites the Judge to dine with Him.
They enter the Dining Room and order a delicious Soup made of [BLANK] and [BLANK] roast; [BLANK] stuffed with [BLANK] and a fine [BLANK] with dressing made of [BLANK].
In addition, the waiter brought them [BLANK] which George relished highly.

For Dessert they had [BLANK] and [BLANK] and after this most satisfactory Repast, George excused himself and again taking the Elevator, returned to his Room, and feeling as tired as [BLANK] he fell asleep and dreamed the following remarkable dream:-
He dreamed he was riding through a crowded street in a beautiful Motor Car with Daniel Webster and Christopher Columbus who were talking of the latest thing in Radio Sets.
George said he had one at Mt. Vernon which looked like [BLANK] and he was getting fine Results.

Christopher said that his dear friend Queen Isabella of Spain had [BLANK] which she was using instead of a Radio, but her great Trouble with it was that when [BLANK] got in the way, Everything sounded like [BLANK] and [BLANK] having a fight.
They were just passing the Woolworth Building when Daniel saw an Airplane sailing around the top of the Tower.
Daniel said it reminded him of [BLANK] but George said it looked more like [BLANK] to him.
They were held up at the Corner by a Traffic Cop who resembled [BLANK] rather than [BLANK].
The Policeman, who recognized them, remarked 'Why, you have just missed Tom Thumb and his wife; they went by a few minutes ago in a twelve cylinder Flivver!'
All expressed regrets at not catching a glimpse once more of their two old friends, and on they hurried.
In the next Block a big Fire was in progress and it burned with such Rapidity that [BLANK] and [BLANK] were destroyed.
To aid in extinguishing the Flames a Fireman took [BLANK] and [BLANK] which helped in quenching the Fire.
While they were standing there [BLANK] whizzed by George's Ear, which almost decapitated [BLANK] he was holding in his Hand.
Next they visit a shop on Fifth Avenue and ask to see [BLANK].
'Sorry, we are all out of them but we have [BLANK] which would answer the purpose very well,' was the courteous reply of the young lady behind the counter.
The dream then changes and George finds himself in the U.S. Naval Station, where John Paul Jones is trying out his new deep-sea submarine.
George discovers [BLANK] in the Water and asks Paul what he uses that for.
'Why,' said Paul, 'that is [BLANK] and not [BLANK].'
Before entering the Sub they discover Thomas Edison and Paul Revere coming toward them.
'Who are you looking for?' said George.
'We are trying to find [BLANK] and [BLANK].'
'Not here,' said George, 'but I saw [BLANK] chasing [BLANK] down by that Radio Tower and you will also find [BLANK] they had with them!'
George then suggested that they test out the Sub and so they went aboard.
They descended to about 98 feet and, through the periscope, George saw a [BLANK] and a [BLANK] being devoured by a gigantic Cod Fish which seemed to be enjoying his Meal.
As the Sub rose to the surface they saw rapidly approaching them the new Super-Dreadnaught 'New Jersey' on the lookout for Capt. Kidd.
Now again the scene changes, and George dreams that he is on the Golf Links playing a foursome with Andrew Carnegie, Nick Carter and Pocahontas.
George said he understood that Andrew had paid fifty billion Marks for one Golf Ball, but Nick said 'Oh No, it was [BLANK] that Andrew had purchased!'
At the ninth hole Miss Pocahontas discovered [BLANK] hanging on a tree a few yards away and exclaimed. 'Now, if John Smith were only here, he would be delighted to get it for me.'
George replied, 'I can get [BLANK] or [BLANK] for you but not [BLANK].'
The Game finished, Miss Pocahontas being the winner, all repaired to the Club House for Lunch.
George called a waiter who was passing and asked him to bring [BLANK] as well as the menu.
After looking the latter over very carefully, he found that it was written in French and that he couldn't read a word of it.
However, he selected [BLANK] to start with, with [BLANK] to follow, after which he had [BLANK] well done with [BLANK] au gratin.
For Dessert he ordered [BLANK] and [BLANK], neither of which he cared much about.
He, however, thoroughly enjoyed [BLANK] which he lighted with [BLANK].
That afternoon George had an appointment with John McGraw to visit the Ball Park where Christopher Columbus' Sailors were playing an exhibition game with the Jail College Team.
To enter the Park one had to have [BLANK] to hand to the officer at the Gate.
George, not having one, gave instead [BLANK].
Such a crowd as was there!
Some had [BLANK] in their hands which they were waving constantly, while others were throwing [BLANK] at each other.
The first man at the Bat seized [BLANK] and made a Crack at the Ball but failed to hit it.
He threw his Bat at [BLANK] which landed there.
The crowd roared!
The batter, however, became so enraged that he snatched [BLANK] lying near at hand and with a mighty swing of his arm flung it at the Umpire.
In the next row but one, George saw Betsy Ross waving [BLANK].
She had her Ukulele and [BLANK] with her and was very proud of [BLANK] she had on a String.
She seemed to be enjoying the game very much.
Sitting beside her was a gentleman who looked very much like Abraham Lincoln, who had [BLANK] on the end of his Cane.
Once more the dream Picture changes and George finds himself in an Airplane with Mary Pickford, Douglas Fairbanks and Charlie Chaplin.
The operator being 'Safety First'-Harold Lloyd, they amble along at about 190 Miles an Hour, when they discover [BLANK] in the Distance.
'It looks to me mighty like [BLANK],' Mary said.
'It is [BLANK],' said George, but Doug replied, 'No, it seems to me more like [BLANK].'
When they came within hailing distance they discovered to their great surprise it was [BLANK].
Charlie then remarked, 'If that was [BLANK] and we had it, we all would look like [BLANK].'
After making a nose Dive to the 860 foot Level, they slow down to about fifty Miles an Hour, and Charlie takes Harold's place as operator.
'Now don't try any Monkey Shines with this Machine or we will be in Kingdom Come before we know it,' says Harold.
The airplane runs as smoothly as [BLANK] in Fly Time.
Suddenly there appeared [BLANK] on the horizon, but they do not even hesitate.

Eventually they arrive at their Destination near Washington, and after a successful Landing, all express Delight at the expertness of the operators and present them with [BLANK] for their wonderful Ability.
From the Landing Field, George invites his Friends to visit the White House, and as it is only a short walk this is soon accomplished.
Passing into the House, George misses [BLANK] which has stood for so many years in the Corner.
In its Place was [BLANK] which caused much merriment.
Charlie, as usual tumbles over [BLANK] which was just beyond [BLANK] in the large Hall.
Mary laughed so heartily that she fell on [BLANK] which was lying on the Rug.
After a short Stay at the White House, they visit the Capitol, where they run across Henry Ford with [BLANK] on a String.
He said he was looking for a Job in the City and hoped to land Something soon.
He remarked that he would like to be President or something like that!
The dream changes again and George finds himself speeding toward Chicago on the 20th Century Limited.
He discovers to his great surprise [BLANK] and [BLANK] across the aisle.
The conductor comes through the train and George hands him [BLANK].
'I want your ticket,' exclaims the conductor, 'and not [BLANK]!'
George makes a thorough search and finds [BLANK] in his pocket which he presents to the conductor.
'All right, this will do,' says that official.
Then suddenly there is an awful crash, the brakes grind, there is a terrible roar, and everything seems to go to pieces.
GEORGE WAKES UP!
